channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-07-04
item.description:
Our open-source research community has seen exciting progress across multiple projects. In the scienceverse/metacheck project, Lakens made foundational additions to the `data_check` branch, laying the groundwork for further development of datacheck modules. Meanwhile, giladfeldman/docpluck saw significant updates to its 'main' branch, including documentation triage, residual fixes, and improvements to the render function. Notably, the removal of the sign-up feature in JamieCummins/regcheck suggests a potential shift in focus or approach. As our projects continue to evolve, we celebrate the connections and collaborations that drive our community forward, and we look forward to seeing how these developments intersect and inform one another in the future. With these updates, our community is poised for continued growth and innovation.

62 commits across 6 active repositories this week.

Dormant repos revived: statcheck

Most active:

docpluck30 commits
regcheck18 commits
escicheck6 commits
metacheck4 commits
statcheck2 commits

item.title: metacheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
scienceverse/metacheck

The `data_check` branch was the focus of the recent work, with additions made by Lakens. As stated, "additions" were made, but the specifics of these additions are not detailed in the commit message. A "first version of datacheck modules" was also created on the `data_check` branch, marking the initial implementation of these modules. The `data_check` branch is where all the changes took place, with no other branches mentioned in the commits. The work appears to be foundational, setting the stage for further development of the datacheck modules.

1 commit(s) today:

  • 342b25c [data_check] additions — Lakens
  • bd8c890 [data_check] first version of datacheck modules — Lakens
  • 41a54be [data_check] prevent double crossref api, log errors — Lakens
  • bb7a5ee [data_check] updated ref_accuracy, validated. Minor fixes to reg_check and ref_consistency — Lakens
  • 6937e9c [data_check] Merge exact_p_zero_warning: flag p-values reported as exactly zero — Lakens
  • 3c854b3 [data_check] Rewrite OSF prereg extraction to schema-driven dispatch — Lakens
  • 32659a9 [main] Point home page text to the Metacheck Manual — Lakens
  • d6a7f12 [main] Replace Get Started/Reference/Articles navbar with Metacheck Manual link — Lakens
  • b8ac461 [data_check] Fix coi_check/funding_check cold-start failure — Lakens
  • de41849 [data_check] Add R/import-papers.R so dev installs cleanly — Lakens
  • ed8c8d3 [data_check] Merge pull request #317 from scienceverse/papers_databases — Lisa DeBruine
  • e077134 [data_check] Merge pull request #316 from scienceverse/ethics_check — Lisa DeBruine
  • a40d7db [data_check] Merge pull request #309 from scienceverse/prereg_regcheck — Lisa DeBruine
  • 1fb2bab [data_check] Merge pull request #318 from scienceverse/fix/code-check-paperlist — Lisa DeBruine
  • 8cbef44 [data_check] Fix code_check crash and missing paper_id on paper lists — Lakens
  • b7afa6a [data_check] add functions to load paper databases from scienceverse/papers — Lakens
  • 25c15a6 [main] update metacheck page to fix #314 — Lakens
  • 73736b0 [data_check] Fix create_report_app() to report_app() in Get Started vignette — Daniel Lakens
  • 00d7953 [data_check] add ethics_check + bug fix — Lakens
  • 2b8a367 [new_modules] Revert "fix app instruction #314" — Lakens

View all commits →

item.title: statcheck: 2 commits today
item.description:
MicheleNuijten/statcheck

The `feature-non-apa-newer` branch was worked on, where a merge was performed from the same branch of the `https://github.com/MicheleNuijten/statcheck` repository. In the `feature-non-apa-newer` branch, "admin updates" were made, as per the commit message. No other branches were modified in this set of commits. The merge commit combined changes from the remote `feature-non-apa-newer` branch into the local version. The nature of the "admin updates" is not specified beyond the commit message.

2 commit(s) today:

  • 50ab5e6 [feature-non-apa-newer] Merge branch 'feature-non-apa-newer' of https://github.com/MicheleNuijten/statcheck into feature-non-apa-newer — MicheleNuijten
  • ffba7eb [feature-non-apa-newer] admin updates — MicheleNuijten

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →

item.title: docpluck: 18 commits today
item.description:
giladfeldman/docpluck

Work was done on the 'main' branch, where documentation triage was performed, recording the run-3 outcome, resolving W0l ×-as-3 residuals, and rescoping efendic headings to B6. Additionally on the 'main' branch, a fix was made to recover 2 residual ×-as-3 prose shapes that W0k can't reach, related to efendic, as part of version 2.4.116. The 'main' branch also saw a fix to the render function, specifically stripping an affiliation remnant that was orphaned in the Abstract body, related to chandrashekar, as part of version 2.4.115. No other branches were worked on, with all changes occurring on the 'main' branch. The commits addressed specific issues, including resolving residuals, recovering prose shapes, and stripping orphaned affiliation remnants. Only the 'main' branch was modified in these commits.

18 commit(s) today:

  • 56f60b5 [main] docs(triage): record run-3 outcome — W0l ×-as-3 residuals resolved (v2.4.116); efendic headings rescoped to B6 — Gilad Feldman
  • a4f8796 [main] fix(glyph): recover 2 residual ×-as-3 prose shapes W0k can't reach (efendic) + v2.4.116 — Gilad Feldman
  • e140cf1 [main] fix(render): strip affiliation remnant orphaned in Abstract body (chandrashekar) + v2.4.115 — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: escicheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
giladfeldman/escicheck

The `main` branch was updated with a commit that synchronizes it with the `escicheckapp` repository at version `ec9eb9f`, which corresponds to `effectcheck` version `0.6.13`, as stated in the commit message "sync from escicheckapp@ec9eb9f (effectcheck v0.6.13)". This commit is associated with issue `#26`. No other branches were worked on in this set of commits. The specific change is a synchronization with a specific version of `effectcheck`, which is now at version `0.6.13` in the `main` branch. The commit message does not specify what changes were made in `effectcheck` version `0.6.13`, only that the `main` branch now matches this version.

1 commit(s) today:

  • 7ec3c89 [main] sync from escicheckapp@ec9eb9f (effectcheck v0.6.13) (#26) — Gilad Feldman
  • e09dbc9 [main] sync from escicheckapp@edb3c7b (effectcheck v0.6.12) (#25) — Gilad Feldman
  • bedc262 [main] sync from escicheckapp@928a5bc (effectcheck v0.6.11) (#24) — Gilad Feldman
  • cc5cfd0 [main] sync from escicheckapp@1d4c2dd (effectcheck v0.6.9) (#23) — Gilad Feldman
  • 75ba643 [main] sync from escicheckapp@26f8430 (effectcheck v0.6.8) (#22) — Gilad Feldman
  • a154bdb [main] sync from escicheckapp@57a791d (effectcheck v0.6.7) (#21) — Gilad Feldman
  • 244b2d8 [main] sync from escicheckapp@f37b5a1 (effectcheck v0.6.6) (#20) — Gilad Feldman
  • a588210 [main] sync from escicheckapp@bd6f007 (effectcheck v0.6.5) (#19) — Gilad Feldman
  • 1d48c4e [main] sync from escicheckapp@dec37b1 (effectcheck v0.6.4) (#18) — Gilad Feldman
  • 4be3331 [main] sync from escicheckapp@f52d7ce (effectcheck v0.6.3) (#17) — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →
